heading: Compensation to victims of international terrorism
label: 20106 Compensation to victims of international terrorism

children.heading: International terrorism
children.label: 1 International terrorism
children.text: The term “international terrorism” has the meaning given the term in .section 2331 of title 18

children.heading: National of the United States
children.label: 2 National of the United States
children.text: The term “national of the United States” has the meaning given the term in .section 1101(a) of title 8

children.label: i
children.text: suffered direct physical or emotional injury or death as a result of international terrorism occurring on or after , with respect to which an investigation or civil or criminal prosecution was ongoing after ; andOctober 23, 1983April 24, 1996

children.label: ii
children.text: as of the date on which the international terrorism occurred, was a national of the United States or an officer or employee of the United States Government.

children.heading: Incompetent, incapacitated, or deceased victims
children.label: B Incompetent, incapacitated, or deceased victims
children.text: In the case of a victim who is less than 18 years of age, incompetent, incapacitated, or deceased, a family member or legal guardian of the victim may receive the compensation under this section on behalf of the victim.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: C Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, in no event shall an individual who is criminally culpable for the terrorist act or mass violence receive any compensation under this section, either directly or on behalf of a victim.

children.heading: Award of compensation
children.label: b Award of compensation
children.text: The Director may use the emergency reserve referred to in to carry out a program to compensate victims of acts of international terrorism that occur outside the United States for expenses associated with that victimization. The amount of compensation awarded to a victim under this subsection shall be reduced by any amount that the victim received in connection with the same act of international terrorism under title VIII of the Omnibus Diplomatic Security and Antiterrorism Act of 1986.section 20101(d)(5)(A) of this title
an explanation of the procedures for filing and processing of applications for compensation;
a description of the procedures and policies instituted to promote public awareness about the program;

children.label: A
children.text: the number of applications for compensation submitted;

children.label: B
children.text: the number of applications approved and the amount of each award;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of applications denied and the reasons for the denial;

children.label: D
children.text: the average length of time to process an application for compensation; and

children.label: E
children.text: the number of applications for compensation pending and the estimated future liability of the program; and
an analysis of future program needs and suggested program improvements.
